skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate still images with Google Nano Banana 2 through the RunComfy CLI. The skill documents model selection, prompt structure, resolution options, web grounding, and output handling for text-to-image workflows.
skill.summary: Generate Nano Banana 2 images through RunComfy with model-specific prompting guidance.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks as shell execution, plus hardcoded URLs and token-file references. Review found no prompt injection or malicious code, but the skill intentionally invokes a third-party CLI, sends prompts to RunComfy endpoints, and depends on local token storage, so it should publish with a security warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to install and run the RunComfy CLI, including `npx skills add`, `npm i -g @runcomfy/cli`, `runcomfy login`, and `runcomfy run`. This is expected for the skill, but it executes external software and should be trusted before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill behavior. The risk is legitimate dependency and command execution, not shell injection in the Markdown itself.

medium_findings.title: Prompt Data Sent To External Model API
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that the CLI posts prompt JSON to the RunComfy Model API and downloads generated outputs from RunComfy domains. This is necessary for image generation, but user prompts and optional web-grounded context leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The outbound API behavior is directly described in the skill. It is expected functionality, but it has clear privacy and network-dependency impact.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Token Storage
low_findings.description: The security section states that `runcomfy login` stores an API token under the user config directory with owner-only permissions. This is not malicious code, but users should understand where credentials are stored.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permission mode are explicitly documented. No evidence shows the skill itself reads or exfiltrates the token.

low_findings.title: Markdown Formatting False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static external-command, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance hits are caused by Markdown backticks, model prose, option names, and numeric values. No Ruby backtick execution, cryptographic operation, or host reconnaissance appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are prose, tables, or fenced shell examples in a Markdown file. They do not execute by themselves, and the suspected cryptographic and reconnaissance terms are ordinary model documentation.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Link Flagged As Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The `../nano-banana-edit` reference is a Markdown link to a sibling skill, not filesystem traversal code. No file read or write operation uses this relative path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a plain Markdown link. There is no executable path handling or user-controlled filesystem access at that location.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on command examples that install and invoke RunComfy tooling. Agents should avoid passing unsanitized shell fragments and should prefer the documented JSON input boundary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI usage is explicit, and the skill documents a JSON-string boundary. The remaining risk is operational trust in the CLI and command execution environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Endpoint Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow submits model requests to RunComfy and downloads results from RunComfy domains. Users should not send sensitive prompts unless that service is approved for their data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoints and download domains are named in the skill. The behavior is expected, but network transfer is confirmed.

content.value_statement: Design teams need fast image drafts without rewriting model parameters each time. This skill routes text-to-image prompts through RunComfy with Nano Banana 2 settings, examples, and safety guidance.
actual_capabilities: Runs the RunComfy Nano Banana 2 text-to-image endpoint from documented CLI commands.
Explains supported aspect ratios, resolutions, output formats, seeds, and batch counts.
Guides prompt writing for subject-first descriptions and quoted in-image text.
Shows when to enable web search for current events or real entities.
Documents model selection tradeoffs against Nano Banana Pro, GPT Image 2, Flux 2, and Seedream.
Describes RunComfy token storage, outbound endpoints, and generated-file size limits.
limitations: This endpoint creates still images only and does not generate video.
Each request supports a maximum of four images.
Image editing is outside this skill and requires the sibling edit endpoint.
Use of RunComfy requires an account, CLI installation, and network access.

use_cases.title: Draft social campaign visuals
use_cases.description: Create several low-resolution vertical or square options, then promote the best prompt to a higher resolution.

use_cases.title: Prepare product concept cards
use_cases.description: Generate clean product cards with quoted label text, controlled framing, and repeatable seeds for review rounds.

use_cases.title: Prototype current-event artwork
use_cases.description: Use optional web grounding when the image prompt depends on a recent event or public entity.

prompt_templates.title: Quick image draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single default image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to create one 1K PNG image of [subject] in [setting], with [style] lighting and clear composition.

prompt_templates.title: Batch social variants
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for rapid concept comparison.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to generate four 9:16 draft images for [platform]. Keep the subject [subject], vary composition, and use 0.5K resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Text-forward brand asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for images with readable text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 16:9 product card with [product]. The headline reads "[exact text]" in [font style], placed [location], with [color and background].

prompt_templates.title: Seeded final refinement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for reproducible final output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refine this Nano Banana 2 concept at 2K using seed [seed]. Preserve the main composition, improve [details], and keep aspect ratio [ratio].

output_examples.input: A marketer asks for four vertical wellness campaign thumbnails with clear headline text.
output_examples.output: The skill selects a 9:16 batch workflow, uses quoted headline text, keeps resolution at draft quality, and returns generated image files in the chosen output directory.

output_examples.input: A designer asks for a final product hero image with the same layout as the best draft.
output_examples.output: The skill uses the documented seed field, raises resolution to 2K, keeps the same aspect ratio, and narrows the prompt to composition and lighting refinements.

output_examples.input: An editor asks for a visual tied to a recent public event.
output_examples.output: The skill enables web grounding, warns about added latency and cost, and routes the prompt through the RunComfy model endpoint.

best_practices: Keep prompts subject-first, then add action, environment, style, camera, and output constraints.
Quote exact in-image text and specify placement, font style, and contrast.
Use 0.5K for exploration, 1K for normal drafts, and 2K or 4K only for finals.
anti_patterns: Do not use this text-to-image endpoint when the task requires preserving a specific uploaded subject.
Do not combine many conflicting styles in one prompt.
Do not enable web search unless the prompt needs recent or real-world context.

faq.question: Does this skill create images directly inside Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to call the local RunComfy CLI, which sends the request to RunComfy.

faq.question: What model endpoint does it use?
Can it edit an existing image?
faq.answer: No. The skill points users to the sibling Nano Banana Edit skill for image editing workflows.

faq.question: When should web search be enabled?
faq.answer: Enable it only for prompts that depend on recent events or real public entities.

faq.question: Where are credentials stored?
faq.answer: The skill says `runcomfy login` stores a token under `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with owner-only permissions.

faq.question: What output formats are documented?
faq.answer: The skill documents PNG, JPEG, and WebP outputs, with PNG as the default.
